output:
# Research Analysis: Reliance-Confounded Learning Gain (RCLG)

Here is a high-level summarized insight of a research field Machine Learning.

The source paper, "(X)AI-Based Learning Systems" (ECIS 2024 [28]), sits in the
KIT human-AI complementarity lineage and treats explainable AI not as a one-off
decision aid but as a TEACHER that should leave novices more competent, reading a
headline gain as evidence that competence was acquired. We name the load-bearing
flaw the Reliance-Confounded Learning Gain (RCLG): the headline scalar - assisted
post-test accuracy, or the agreement-based appropriate-reliance rate - is
measured WHILE the AI and its explanation are still present, so the
"measured-while-assisted equals learned" assumption conflates three components:
(C1) durable, AI-INDEPENDENT competence the learner keeps once the aid is
withdrawn, (C2) transient explanation-scaffolding / reliance that evaporates on
removal and can become a net-negative crutch, and (C3) a SEPARATELY LABELED
scaffold-by-difficulty interaction in which the explanation builds reliance
precisely on the items the novice cannot solve alone. We promote the single
assisted scalar to a transfer-controlled, reliance-decomposed C1/C2/C3 profile,
measured with an AI-removed delayed unaided post-test as the C1 channel plus
placebo / counterfactual-explanation arms and an AI-present-versus-removed
factor, estimated by a crossed mixed-effects (GLMM) variance decomposition over
participant x item x explanation-type. The profile is bound to the source by a
by-construction collapse: disabling the AI-removed channel, supplying the real
(non-placebo) explanation, and marginalizing to a single grand-mean readout
recovers the original assisted-accuracy / appropriate-reliance scalar as
recomputed within floating-point tolerance, so the new object strictly CONTAINS
the old number rather than competing with it.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1. Background and Current State of the Art

The modern explainable-AI (XAI) program grew out of a decision-support premise:
if a model exposes WHY it predicts, a human can decide WHEN to follow it, and the
human-AI team should beat either party alone. Adadi and Berrada's 2018 survey [1]
codified this interpretability turn and its motivating promise of calibrated,
transparent assistance. Early human-subjects work made the dependent variable
explicit by instrumenting explanations for their effect on trust and reliance in
real decision settings - clinical decision support (Bussone, Stumpf and
O'Sullivan 2015 [2]) and machine-assisted human prediction (Lai and Tan 2019 [3],
on deception detection). The field's aspirational target was crystallized by
Bansal et al. 2021 [4] as COMPLEMENTARY team performance - the whole exceeding its
parts - measured on the team's decisions while the AI and its explanation remain
on screen.

That promise proved fragile. The same explanations meant to calibrate reliance
were found to INCREASE it indiscriminately: Bansal et al. [4] showed explanations
can raise human acceptance of WRONG AI advice, and Bucinca, Malaya and Gajos 2021
[5] showed that only cognitive-forcing interventions - which deliberately add
friction - curb this overreliance, at a cost to user satisfaction. A meta-analysis
(Schemmer, Hemmer, Nitsche, Kuehl and Vossing 2022 [6]) found the utility of
explanations for human-AI decision quality to be, at best, inconsistent, and an
IS-venue study tied explanations directly to AUTOMATION BIAS (Schemmer, Kuehl,
Benz and Satzger 2022 [7]). The lesson the subfield drew was that raw reliance is
the wrong target; the right target is APPROPRIATE reliance.

Appropriate reliance was then conceptualized and operationalized as a two-sided
behavior - follow correct AI advice, reject incorrect advice - and measured with
agreement-based metrics (Schemmer, Hemmer, Kuehl, Bartelt and Satzger 2022 [8];
Schemmer, Kuehl, Benz, Bartelt and Satzger 2023 [9]). This is now the dominant
dependent variable for human-AI decision studies. Note the structural feature that
becomes central below: the appropriate-reliance rate is DEFINITIONALLY an
AI-present quantity. It is computed from whether the human agrees or disagrees
with AI advice at decision time; with no AI advice on screen there is nothing to
agree with, hence nothing to measure. Reliance, by construction, is read while the
crutch is still in place.

A parallel line reframes the AI not as a one-off decision aid but as a TEACHER or
TRAINER that should leave the novice more capable. Machine teaching formalized how
to choose examples that make a learner converge quickly (Cakmak and Lopes 2012
[10]), and interactive machine teaching extended this to human learners acquiring
fine-grained visual categories (Johns, Mac Aodha and Brostow 2015 [11]; Mac Aodha
et al. 2018 [12], which teaches categories with visual explanations). The
aspiration of AI-as-teacher is stated explicitly in education-facing work (Edwards
and Cheok 2018 [13]) and in the augmentation-versus-automation framing of AI and
human discretion (Bullock, Young and Wang 2020 [14]): augmentation is supposed to
build human capability, not merely substitute for it. Comparative studies ask who
learns patterns faster, human or model (Kuehl et al. 2022 [15]), and intelligent
tutoring systems personalize pedagogical interventions (Kochmar et al. 2022 [16]).
The source paper, "(X)AI-Based Learning Systems" (ECIS 2024 [28]), sits squarely
in this lineage: it treats (X)AI as a system that TEACHES novices, and reads a
headline gain as evidence that competence has been acquired.

Because explanations are themselves information to be processed, a third
literature studies their COGNITIVE LOAD. Cognitive load theory (Sweller 1988 [17])
and the notion of cognitive overload (Kirsh 2000 [18]) predict that richer
explanations can consume working-memory resources that would otherwise serve the
task. Empirically, XAI visualizations measurably move user cognitive load and
confidence (Hudon et al. 2021 [19]; Herm 2023 [20]), and the effect is modulated
by individual cognitive style (Kozhevnikov 2007 [21]; Kirby, Moore and Schofield
1988 [22]). This matters here for one specific reason: explanation DOSE (none,
minimal, full) can change reliance and confidence WITHOUT changing what the
learner could do unaided - the precondition for a dose-response dissociation
between an assisted score and durable competence.

The decisive prior result the XAI subfield has NOT ported lives in education and
the learning sciences, which settled decades ago that PERFORMANCE-DURING-TRAINING
and DURABLE LEARNING are different things and can move in opposite directions.
"Desirable difficulties" (Bjork and Bjork 2011 [23]) show that conditions which
DEPRESS in-the-moment performance can IMPROVE long-term retention and transfer,
and conversely that help which inflates immediate performance can leave less
durable learning. The "assistance dilemma" (Koedinger and Aleven 2007 [24]) names
exactly the give-versus-withhold-help trade-off and shows that too much assistance
yields shallow, non-transferring performance. The "expertise reversal effect"
(Kalyuga, Ayres, Chandler and Sweller 2003 [25]) shows guidance that helps novices
becomes redundant or even harmful as competence grows. The methodological
consequence the field institutionalized is scaffolding-and-fading plus a
GOLD-STANDARD readout: a DELAYED, UNAIDED transfer / retention test taken after
support is withdrawn. A related veridicality concern - that the "ground truth" and
expert know-what used to build and certify AI may not be true (Lebovitz,
Lifshitz-Assaf and Levina 2021 [26]) - reinforces why the CONTENT of an
explanation, not merely its presence, must be controlled.

These two bodies of work have not met. XAI appropriate-reliance measurement reads
its verdict WHILE the AI and explanation are present; the learning sciences insist
the verdict be read AFTER they are removed, on a delayed unaided transfer test.
The gap is not that either result is unknown - it is that the AI-removed, delayed,
unaided readout sits structurally OUTSIDE the appropriate-reliance measurement
frame, and the education distinction has never been expressed as a decomposition
of an (X)AI system's reported gain. Even the most apt empirical vehicle shows the
miss: Leichtmann et al. 2023 [27] teaches mushroom foraging with XAI - an ideal
learning task with clean ground truth - yet reports assisted decision improvement,
not an AI-removed delayed unaided outcome.

This is the empty cell. Every result above measures learning or reliance WHILE the
AI and its explanation are present, and reads the resulting scalar - post-test
accuracy or the agreement-based appropriate-reliance rate - as durable,
AI-independent competence. NO prior work decomposes an (X)AI learning system's
headline gain into (C1) durable, AI-INDEPENDENT competence retained on an unaided
delayed post-test, (C2) transient explanation-scaffolding / reliance that vanishes
when the AI is removed and can be a net-negative crutch, and (C3) a SEPARATELY
LABELED scaffold-by-difficulty interaction in which the explanation builds
reliance precisely on the items the novice cannot solve alone - with a
by-construction collapse back to the assisted scalar as recomputed within
floating-point tolerance when the AI-removed channel is disabled, the real
explanation is supplied, and the decomposition is marginalized to a single
grand-mean readout. The Reliance-Confounded Learning Gain (RCLG) gap fills exactly
that cell.

## 2. Key Findings and Analysis

**Spine-Fit Gate verdict: EVAL-CONFOUND decomposition.** The source is an
empirical human-subjects MEASUREMENT paper: it instruments novices interacting
with an (X)AI system and reads a learning verdict - "(X)AI teaches / trains
novices to become more competent" - off a reported behavioral scalar. It does NOT
propose a new algorithm or system to extend. Per the team Spine-Fit Gate, a paper
that reports a metric and reads a conclusion off it gets the eval-confound
decomposition spine, not a mechanism reframe. So we audit the measurement rather
than re-engineer a method, and the named gap is the Reliance-Confounded Learning
Gain (RCLG).

**The load-bearing assumption: "measured-while-assisted equals learned."** The
headline scalar - assisted post-test accuracy, or the agreement-based
appropriate-reliance rate, both measured with the AI and its explanation PRESENT -
is read as durable, AI-independent competence the novice has acquired. But the
assisted scalar is, in part, a live readout of the scaffold itself: a richer or
higher-dose explanation can raise the assisted number specifically on the items
the learner cannot do alone, inflating apparent teaching while building DEPENDENCE
rather than competence. The point is sharpest for appropriate reliance, which is
definitionally an AI-present object (no AI advice means nothing to rely on), so
reporting it as evidence of learning is nearly tautological unless an AI-removed
unaided readout is added.

**The decomposition (assisted scalar into C1, C2, and the separately labeled C3 interaction).** We split the single
reported number into three labeled components mapped to this domain:
- **C1 (signal-attributable) = durable, AI-INDEPENDENT competence**: the portion
  the learner RETAINS on an unaided delayed post-test after the AI and explanation
  are withdrawn (genuine learning / transfer) - the only thing a "learning system"
  is entitled to claim.
- **C2 (confound-attributable) = transient explanation-scaffolding / reliance**:
  the portion that exists ONLY because the AI plus explanation are present at test
  time and vanishes the moment they are removed; it can be net-negative (a crutch
  that leaves the learner worse than self-study).
- **C3 (interaction, SEPARATELY LABELED) = scaffold-by-difficulty crutch effect**:
  the explanation builds reliance precisely on the items the novice cannot solve
  alone (learned helplessness on the hard cases) - a participant x item x
  explanation-type interaction, never folded into C1 or C2.

**Pressure-test of rival reframes (why RCLG wins).** Three alternative readings
each turn out to be either already inside the frame or controlled by design:
- *Alt-A: "novices just lack the skill - this is pre-existing aptitude, not
  teaching."* That IS C1, and we hold it fixed by randomized assignment of the
  explanation-type factor plus the AI-removed delayed unaided readout, so
  pre-existing ability cannot masquerade as a teaching effect; cross-sectional
  accuracy without a baseline is exactly the confound the design removes.
- *Alt-B: "it is just a harder test once the AI is gone."* Handled within-study by
  counterbalanced, difficulty-matched PARALLEL item forms across occasions and the
  within-subject AI-present-versus-removed contrast, so a test-difficulty shift
  cannot be mistaken for lost competence.
- *Alt-C: "explanations genuinely help - you are denying that."* We are NOT denying
  it. RCLG isolates the durable part (C1) from the scaffolding part (C2 / C3)
  instead of crediting the whole assisted gain to learning; if the help is real
  and durable it shows up in C1, and the placebo legs return null. RCLG wins
  because it is the only reading that both contains the source claim as a special
  case AND yields a falsifiable separation the assisted scalar cannot.

**Baseline ladder B1-B4.** Each rung strictly adds correction:
- **B1 (collapse target = source recomputed):** AI-present + real explanation,
  immediate, full dose; assisted accuracy / appropriate-reliance computed our way.
  Equals the collapse unit-test output within floating-point tolerance; reproduces
  the source's reported measurement within reproduction tolerance.
- **B2 (AI-removed unaided IMMEDIATE correction):** withdraw the AI and explanation
  at the immediate test, measure unaided - the single most important fix and the
  first separation of durable C1 from transient C2.
- **B3 (full transfer-controlled, reliance-decomposed profile):** AI-removed
  unaided DELAYED post-test as the C1 readout, crossed with real-vs-placebo
  explanation arms and the AI-present-vs-removed factor; the crossed GLMM emits C1,
  C2, and the separately labeled C3. This is the full operating object.
- **B4 (ORACLE gold-pedagogy ceiling - UPPER BOUND ONLY):** gold instruction
  (human-expert tutoring, full worked solutions, or training under
  ground-truth-correct error-free explanations) read on the same unaided delayed
  post-test, bounding the maximum durable C1 the channel could ever deliver. It is
  an upper bound only; the within-study real-vs-placebo and AI-present-vs-removed
  contrasts remain the PRIMARY estimator, and the secondary-domain (CUB)
  generalization gap is likewise an upper bound, not primary.

**Falsifiability triad.**
1. *By-construction collapse:* with the AI-removed channel disabled, the real
   (non-placebo) explanation supplied, and the decomposition marginalized to a
   single grand-mean readout, the profile collapses to the original assisted
   scalar as recomputed within floating-point tolerance; if it does not, the
   construction is wrong (unit-testable).
2. *DUAL / two-leg placebo (both legs required):* Leg-1 (STRUCTURE-DESTROYING) is a
   content-scrambled explanation matched on surface form, length, and
   cognitive-load footprint but carrying no veridical task signal - if the measured
   durable C1 was real, it must collapse toward zero under Leg-1; Leg-2
   (ABSENT-MECHANISM) removes the AI's predictive advice while keeping an
   explanation-shaped scaffold (or a matched-time self-study control) - C2 must
   drop to approximately zero because there is no AI advice to rely on.
3. *Dose-response monotonicity:* as explanation dose goes none -> minimal -> full,
   the ASSISTED scalar rises monotonically while the UNAIDED delayed C1 fails to
   rise commensurately and/or C2 and C3 rise - a measurable assisted-versus-durable
   DISSOCIATION. The pre-committed refutation is the mirror image: unaided delayed
   C1 tracks the assisted scalar one-for-one, both placebo legs come back null, and
   there is no dose dissociation, in which case the (X)AI learning gain is genuinely
   durable and RCLG is wrong.

**Summary of the collective idea.** (X)AI "learning / teaching" systems certify
novices on a number measured while the AI crutch is still present. RCLG moves the
readout to an AI-removed, unaided, DELAYED transfer test and decomposes the gain
into durable competence (C1), transient reliance-scaffolding (C2), and a
separately labeled scaffold-by-difficulty interaction (C3), estimated by a crossed
GLMM over participant x item x explanation-type, with dual-placebo and
dose-response falsification and a by-construction collapse to the original scalar
as recomputed within floating-point tolerance. The predicted finding - that a
substantial part of the reported gain is reliance, not durable learning, with
richer explanations sometimes a crutch concentrated on the hardest items - turns
appropriate reliance and overreliance from endpoints into components and yields a
reusable measurement-audit instrument for any human-AI learning evaluation.

## 3. Novel Insights and Recommendations

1. **The reported learning gain is an ATTRIBUTION question, not a settled fact.**
   The subgenre's headline - "(X)AI teaches novices" - is read off a scalar
   measured under the aid, which is observationally identical whether the answer is
   durable competence (C1) or a live readout of the scaffold (C2). The
   recommendation is to stop treating the assisted number as a learning verdict and
   start treating it as a quantity to be ATTRIBUTED: decompose it into C1 / C2 / C3
   under an AI-removed delayed unaided readout, and report the profile instead of
   the single number. Because appropriate reliance is definitionally AI-present, the
   durable channel does not exist in current data - it must be collected, and the
   collapse runs only one way (disable the AI-removed channel, zero the placebo arm,
   marginalize, and recover the source scalar as recomputed within floating-point
   tolerance; you cannot start from the source scalar and back out C1).

2. **The contribution is a collapse-anchored C1 / C2 / C3 decomposition, and the
   honest closest prior art is the education near-miss.** Education and the learning
   sciences established decades ago that in-the-moment help can inflate performance
   while depressing durable learning (desirable difficulties [23], the assistance
   dilemma [24], the expertise reversal effect [25]) and institutionalized the
   delayed unaided transfer test. RCLG does not claim that insight as new; its
   novelty is to PORT and OPERATIONALIZE it for (X)AI appropriate-reliance
   measurement - expressing the distinction as a variance decomposition of an (X)AI
   system's headline scalar, naming the expertise-reversal / assistance-dilemma
   intuition as the SEPARATELY LABELED interaction component C3, and binding the
   whole to the source metric through the collapse identity so the new profile
   strictly contains the old number rather than competing with it.

3. **The hardest claim is identifying durable C1 with no ground-truth competence
   label, and it is resolved by design, not by assumption.** There is no oracle for
   "what this novice truly knows," so we do not chase one; we MANUFACTURE the
   missing counterfactual with an AI-removed, unaided, delayed post-test as the C1
   channel, then certify the mechanism with the dual placebo (Leg-1
   structure-destroying drives real C1 toward zero; Leg-2 absent-mechanism drives C2
   toward zero) and the dose-response dissociation. We keep identifiability honest:
   C1 / C2 / C3 are OPERATIONAL design contrasts, not recovered latent traits, and
   because an interaction variance component is the most data-hungry quantity, C3 is
   reported with its interval and may be only BOUNDED at feasible sample sizes
   rather than precisely estimated - never overclaimed.

This is a measurement-audit instrument on low-stakes tasks with consenting
non-expert volunteers; any clinical / high-stakes framing is motivation only, and
the placebo / counterfactual arms carry mild deception. IRB / ethics approval is
secured before data collection and pre-registration, with informed consent and a
debrief guaranteeing no lasting misinformation (no participant leaves believing
scrambled mushroom or medical guidance); the deception is approved under the
documented debrief protocol. Participant demographics (age band, gender, education,
AI-familiarity) are collected under consent and reported in AGGREGATE only - for
sample description and the pre-registered cognitive-style moderation (a moderator,
never deficit framing), with no individual-level identification.

Here are the five core questions:

**[Question 1] - What is the problem?**

When the headline gain reported for an (X)AI-based learning system - the
post-test accuracy or agreement-based appropriate-reliance rate measured while
the AI and its explanation remain present - conflates genuine durable
AI-independent task competence, transient explanation-scaffolding (reliance that
vanishes once the AI is withdrawn), and their interaction (explanation-built
reliance concentrated on exactly the items the novice cannot solve alone), does
promoting that single assisted scalar to a transfer-controlled,
reliance-decomposed learning-gain profile - measured under an AI-removed unaided
delayed post-test together with counterfactual placebo-explanation arms and an
AI-present-versus-removed factor, and decomposed by crossed mixed-effects
variance components over participant, item, and explanation-type into a
durable-competence component C1, a scaffolding-reliance component C2, and a
separately labeled scaffold-by-difficulty interaction component C3, and
constructed so that with the AI-removed channel disabled, the real
(non-placebo) explanation supplied, and the decomposition marginalized to a
single grand-mean readout the profile collapses to the original
assisted-accuracy or appropriate-reliance scalar as recomputed within
floating-point tolerance - reveal that the apparent teaching effect of the
learning system is partly or wholly attributable to transient
reliance-scaffolding and its interaction rather than to genuinely acquired,
AI-independent competence?

**[Question 2] - Why is it interesting and important?**

The stakes are not one paper's effect size; they are the validity of an entire
subgenre's headline claim. RCLG converts "the (X)AI system teaches novices" from
an assertion read off an assisted scalar into a testable decomposition with a
built-in special case that recovers the original number.

1. It corrects a SUBGENRE, not a single study. Any (X)AI learning or training
   result that reports an AI-present post-test or an agreement-based
   appropriate-reliance rate is exposed to the same confound. RCLG provides the
   instrument to re-audit those headline numbers, rather than disputing one of
   them in isolation. The contribution is a measurement frame that travels.

2. It reframes appropriate reliance and overreliance from ENDPOINTS into
   COMPONENTS. The field currently treats appropriate reliance [8, 9] as the thing
   to maximize and overreliance / automation bias [5, 6, 7] as the thing to
   minimize, both measured at AI-present time. RCLG shows the reported learning
   gain can itself be overreliance in disguise (C2), and that part of it
   concentrates on the hardest items (C3) - the components an AI-present reliance
   metric cannot resolve.

3. It ships a REUSABLE audit instrument. The C1/C2/C3 profile is method-agnostic:
   any human-AI learning or training claim can be re-expressed as a profile plus
   the collapse identity that recovers the original assisted scalar, as recomputed, as a special
   case. This makes it a portable measurement check for IS/HCI/ML-eval, not a
   one-task finding tied to a single dataset or model.

4. It is measurement reform centered on the READOUT CONDITION. The reform is a
   single discipline: a number measured while the aid is present cannot, by
   itself, certify AI-independent competence. The collapse identity is what makes
   the reform non-destructive - the original metric survives as the marginalized
   grand-mean special case, so adoption costs nothing in comparability with prior
   results while adding the missing AI-removed channel.

5. The practical stakes are asymmetric and safety-relevant (MOTIVATION ONLY,
   scoped). Mis-certifying dependence as competence is dangerous precisely when a
   novice later acts WITHOUT the AI - the trainee after deployment, the junior
   analyst without the tool, the responder when the system is offline. Clinical /
   triage / finance settings MOTIVATE why the C1-versus-C2 distinction matters;
   they are not deployment claims. The study itself is scoped to low-stakes tasks
   with non-expert volunteers, and any higher-stakes domain is explicitly
   future-work under separate ethics review.

Effect on future research: RCLG supplies a new dependent variable (the decomposed
profile) and a pre-registerable design (AI-removed delayed unaided post-test,
counterfactual placebo-explanation arms, AI-present-versus-removed factor) that
future (X)AI-learning papers can adopt as a reporting norm. It yields a
falsifiable dose-response DISSOCIATION prediction - the assisted score rises with
explanation dose while the unaided delayed C1 fails to rise commensurately - which
turns an abstract critique into a measurable hypothesis. Even a NULL result is
informative: if C2 and C3 are near zero, the subgenre's claims are vindicated
under a strictly stronger test, and the profile becomes the certificate that the
gain was genuine learning rather than scaffolding.

**[Question 3] - Why is it hard?**

The difficulty is structural, not incidental: the quantity we want (durable,
AI-independent competence) is a counterfactual that the source's own measurement
condition can never expose, and every obvious shortcut re-imports the confound it
was meant to remove. Seven concrete obstacles.

**(a) Counterfactual unobservability -- the thing we want to know is never directly
observed.** "What would this novice know WITHOUT the scaffold" is a counterfactual:
for any participant on any item we observe either the assisted outcome or the
unaided outcome, not both at once. This is the fundamental problem of causal
inference applied to learning. Two naive shortcuts provably fail:
- *Assisted post-test alone cannot separate C1 from C2.* With the AI and its
  explanation on-screen, a correct answer can be durable competence the learner
  would keep (C1) OR a live readout of the scaffold that evaporates on withdrawal
  (C2). The single assisted scalar gives no leverage to attribute the answer to
  one versus the other -- they are observationally identical at assisted time.
- *Cross-sectional accuracy confounds prior skill.* Comparing high versus low
  scorers, or comparing groups without randomization and without a baseline,
  attributes to "the system taught them" what may simply be pre-existing aptitude
  or selection. Accuracy is not a learning signal unless prior skill is held fixed.
The only resolution is to MANUFACTURE the counterfactual by design: an AI-removed,
unaided, DELAYED post-test as the C1 channel, within-subject where counterbalanced
items permit, with randomized assignment for the between-subject explanation-type
factor. That readout does not exist in the source data -- it must be collected.

**(b) The appropriate-reliance metric is definitionally AI-present, so the durable
readout sits OUTSIDE the source frame.** The agreement-based appropriate-reliance
rate is computed as (follow-correct-advice + override-incorrect-advice)/total: it
REQUIRES AI advice to exist. Remove the AI and there is nothing to rely on -- the
metric is undefined, not merely lower. Therefore C1 (unaided durable competence)
cannot be recovered by ANY transformation of the appropriate-reliance scalar; it is
a different measurement object in a different condition. Consequence for the
program: B2/B3 are NOT re-analyses of published numbers but NEW data collection
under a measurement condition the source never instrumented. (This is exactly why
the collapse runs the other way -- disabling the AI-removed channel + zeroing the
placebo arm + marginalizing returns us to the source scalar as recomputed within
floating-point tolerance; you cannot start from the source scalar and back out C1.)

**(c) Adding a delayed unaided test imports practice / test-retest, fatigue, and
item-leakage threats.** The delayed post-test is the load-bearing C1 readout, and
it is fragile:
- *Practice / test-retest.* If the same item is seen at immediate and delayed test,
  the delayed score is inflated by memory of THAT item, not durable competence.
  Mitigate with counterbalanced PARALLEL item forms (disjoint items per occasion,
  matched on difficulty), so retest cannot leak.
- *Fatigue / position effects.* Long sessions depress later-measured performance and
  alias onto the test-time factor. Mitigate with session-length caps, breaks,
  randomized item order, and explicit modeling of serial position.
- *Item-leakage / memorization.* If training items reappear at test, we measure
  recall, not transfer. Require disjoint train/test pools PLUS dedicated transfer
  items (novel unseen instances) so C1 reflects generalization.
- *Differential attrition at the ~1 week delay.* Drop-out is non-random; if stronger
  learners return, C1 is biased upward. Mitigate with over-recruitment, completion
  incentives, attrition modeling, and a returners-versus-non-returners check on
  immediate scores.

**(d) The Leg-1 placebo must be matched on cognitive-load, time-on-task, and surface
form so it isolates veridical signal, not effort.** A no-explanation control differs
from a real explanation on many nuisance dimensions at once: presence of
text/highlights, dwell time, mental effort, engagement, arousal. If "real" beats
that control, the gain is unattributable -- it could be veridical task signal OR
simply more time / more engagement. Leg-1 (structure-destroying placebo) must match
the real explanation on surface form (same count of highlighted features, same text
length, same layout), on time-on-task (enforced/matched dwell), and on cognitive-
load footprint (comparable NASA-TLX), while carrying NO veridical task-relevant
signal (feature attributions shuffled across items, or a real explanation belonging
to a DIFFERENT item). Only then does "real minus Leg-1" isolate the veridical-signal
contribution to C1. This is delicate: scrambled explanations can be detectably
incoherent (changing engagement) or can accidentally retain partial signal; a pilot
must verify both load-matching and non-detection before the main run.

**(e) Demand effects, Hawthorne, and deception management.** Participants readily
guess that explanations are "supposed to help" and perform to that hypothesis
(demand characteristics); being observed changes behavior (Hawthorne). Mitigate with
blind arm assignment, neutral framing / cover story, and a BETWEEN-subject
explanation-type factor so no participant can self-compare conditions. The Leg-1
(and counterfactual) arms are mild deception -- participants believe a non-veridical
explanation is real -- which is simultaneously an ethics obligation and a validity
threat: suspicion probes are required, and participants who detect the manipulation
must be flagged/excluded per pre-registration. Debrief must guarantee no lasting
misinformation (no one leaves believing scrambled mushroom or medical guidance).
[Ethics specifics handled under separate RAI review.]

**(f) Identifiability honesty -- C1/C2/C3 are OPERATIONAL contrasts, not latent
traits.** We define the three components strictly by design contrasts, never as
recovered psychological latents:
- C1 = unaided delayed post-test accuracy (the WITHDRAWAL contrast);
- C2 = assisted-minus-unaided gap at matched time (the PRESENCE contrast);
- C3 = participant x item-difficulty x explanation-type interaction variance
  component (the INTERACTION contrast), SEPARATELY LABELED and never folded into
  C1 or C2.
We do NOT claim to recover a person's "true ability" or a "reliance disposition,"
and we do NOT promise clean epistemic-versus-aleatoric or ability-versus-motivation
separation the design cannot deliver. Furthermore, variance-component estimation
needs adequate samples on BOTH crossed dimensions -- enough participants AND enough
items per cell. With too few items or participants, random-effect variances
(especially the three-way interaction) hit boundary estimates (variance = 0) or fail
to converge. A crossed design must be large in both directions, not just in N.

**(g) Power for an INTERACTION (C3) is the hardest part.** Interaction effects carry
smaller standardized magnitudes and larger standard errors than main effects;
detecting a three-way participant x item x explanation-type interaction variance
component needs far more data than detecting C1 or C2 (the textbook rule of thumb is
on the order of 4x the sample for the same standardized effect, and high-order random-
effect variances are worse still). Honest consequence: C3 may be the component we can
only BOUND, not precisely estimate, at feasible N. We therefore report the minimum
detectable C3 a priori (by simulation) and keep all Q5 claims about C3 within what
this power admits -- C3 is reported with its interval and never overclaimed.

**[Question 4] - Why hasn't it been solved before?**

The barrier is not effort. The dominant metric DEFINES the confound out of
existence, and the result that would flag it lives in a different field. Five
concrete barriers, each one explicit:

Barrier 1 (the core barrier): the appropriate-reliance metric is DEFINITIONALLY
AI-present. It is computed from agreement with AI advice at decision time; remove
the AI and there is nothing to rely on, so the metric is undefined. The
AI-removed, unaided readout - the only channel that can isolate durable competence
(C1) - is therefore structurally OUTSIDE the measurement frame the subfield
standardized on [8, 9]. You cannot decompose with an instrument whose zero point
ceases to exist the moment the aid is withdrawn.

Barrier 2: the two literatures are SILOED. The performance-during-training-versus-
durable-learning result, with its delayed unaided transfer test, is settled in
education and the learning sciences (desirable difficulties [23], the assistance
dilemma [24], the expertise reversal effect [25]) but is essentially absent from
HCI/IS XAI evaluation, where the dependent variables are trust, reliance, and
AI-present accuracy [1, 4, 8]. Closing the gap required importing an education
methodology into an XAI measurement - something neither community had occasion to
do.

Barrier 3: delayed UNAIDED transfer tests are COSTLY and RARE. They require
longitudinal sessions, attrition control, counterbalanced item sets to defeat
test-retest practice effects, and within-subject AI-present-versus-removed
contrasts. The cheap single-session assisted post-test is the path of least
resistance - and it is exactly the confounded readout.

Barrier 4: "explanations help" was treated as MONOTONIC. If a richer explanation
is assumed to be strictly more teaching, there is no reason to look for a
dose-response DISSOCIATION in which the assisted score climbs while durable
competence does not. RCLG's dose axis and placebo arms exist precisely to test the
monotonicity that prior work assumed rather than measured.

Barrier 5: no one built the COLLAPSE-IDENTITY profile. Decomposition critiques
usually REPLACE the original metric, which makes them easy to dismiss as a
different measurement. RCLG instead keeps the source assisted scalar as a
by-construction special case - AI-removed channel disabled, real (non-placebo)
explanation supplied, decomposition marginalized to a single grand-mean readout,
recovered as recomputed within floating-point tolerance - so the new profile
strictly CONTAINS the old number rather than competing with it.

How RCLG differs from and improves on each near-miss (distinguishing axes, not
vague claims):

- vs appropriate reliance [8, 9]: SAME data, DIFFERENT readout condition and
  object. Appropriate reliance is an AI-present, agreement-based SCALAR that scores
  reliance behavior; it cannot separate durable competence (C1) from
  scaffolding-reliance (C2) or the scaffold-by-difficulty interaction (C3) because
  all three are measured under the aid. RCLG adds the AI-removed delayed unaided
  channel and a crossed mixed-effects variance decomposition over participant x
  item x explanation-type, and treats the appropriate-reliance scalar as its
  COLLAPSE TARGET, not a rival.

- vs overreliance / automation bias [5, 6, 7]: these diagnose an AI-present ERROR
  pattern (following wrong advice now). RCLG asks the orthogonal question - whether
  the apparent LEARNING is itself transient reliance (C2) once the AI is withdrawn,
  and whether it concentrates on the unsolvable-alone items (C3) - which an
  AI-present overreliance measure cannot reach.

- vs machine teaching [10, 11, 12]: that line optimizes teaching EFFICIENCY (how
  few examples to converge) and often DOES test human learning, but it does not
  decompose a reported gain into reliance components, does not use an AI-removed
  delayed transfer readout as the C1 channel, and has no collapse identity back to
  an assisted scalar. RCLG is a measurement-confound decomposition, not a
  teaching-efficiency method.

- vs the education near-miss [23, 24, 25] (the strongest and closest): education
  ESTABLISHED that in-the-moment help can inflate performance while depressing
  durable learning, and institutionalized the delayed unaided transfer test.
  RCLG's improvement is to PORT and OPERATIONALIZE that distinction for (X)AI
  appropriate-reliance measurement specifically: it expresses the education insight
  as a variance decomposition of an (X)AI system's headline scalar (C1/C2/C3),
  names the expertise-reversal / assistance-dilemma intuition as a SEPARATELY
  LABELED interaction component C3 (reliance built precisely on the items the
  novice cannot solve alone), and binds it to the source metric through the
  collapse identity. Education never targeted an (X)AI explanation or the
  appropriate-reliance construct; XAI never imported the delayed unaided transfer
  logic. RCLG is the join, not a restatement of either side.

- vs cognitive load [17, 18, 19, 20] and veridicality [26]: cognitive-load work
  documents that explanations cost working-memory resources and that style
  moderates this, but it does not connect load to a durable-versus-transient
  decomposition. The veridicality concern [26] motivates controlling explanation
  CONTENT (the placebo / counterfactual arms) but does not by itself separate
  learning from reliance. RCLG consumes both as design inputs rather than treating
  either as the decomposition.

**[Question 5] - What are the key components of my approach and results?**

A controlled, PRE-REGISTERED, human-subjects learning experiment that promotes the
single assisted scalar to the transfer-controlled, reliance-decomposed C1/C2/C3
profile of the locked frame, with a by-construction collapse back to the source
scalar as recomputed within floating-point tolerance.

### Design
- **Type:** controlled, pre-registered (design + GLMM + collapse unit test
  registered before data collection; e.g., OSF / AsPredicted).
- **Factors:**
  - explanation-type {real (X)AI explanation, placebo/counterfactual-scrambled
    (Leg-1), none / no-explanation (Leg-2 anchor)} -- BETWEEN-subject, to avoid
    cross-condition contamination and demand-driven self-comparison;
  - AI-presence-at-test {present (assisted), removed (unaided)} -- WITHIN-subject via
    counterbalanced parallel item forms;
  - test-time {immediate, delayed (~1 week)} -- WITHIN-subject, parallel forms;
  - explanation-dose {none, minimal, full} -- richness/scaffolding intensity,
    realized within the real-explanation arm (dose=none coincides with the
    no-explanation anchor).
- **Counterbalancing:** disjoint, difficulty-matched parallel item sets across
  test occasions kill test-retest/practice; randomized item order controls fatigue/
  position; blind assignment controls demand/Hawthorne.

### Task domains / datasets (real, public)
- **PRIMARY -- mushroom edibility classification.** Precedent: Leichtmann et al.
  2023 [27] (mushroom foraging XAI). Ground-truth labels and categorical features
  from the UCI Mushroom dataset [29] (8124 instances; 22 categorical attributes;
  edible/poisonous). Established XAI-learning task, clear ground truth, low stakes,
  novices have no prior expertise. AI = trained classifier; explanations = feature
  attributions / example-based / counterfactual.
- **SECONDARY (generality / external validity = UPPER BOUND, not primary) --
  fine-grained bird-species identification.** CUB-200-2011 [30] (Caltech-UCSD Birds;
  11788 images, 200 species, part/attribute annotations) for a PERCEPTUAL-expertise
  task. Tests whether the C1/C2/C3 dissociation generalizes beyond categorical
  reasoning; any cross-domain gap is an upper bound only.
- **OPTIONAL higher-salience proxy -- dermoscopy lesion triage.** HAM10000 / ISIC
  [31] (public dermatoscopic images, 7 diagnostic categories) with NON-clinical LAY
  novices and strict RAI scoping: MOTIVATION ONLY, never clinical deployment
  evidence. Included only to show the confound matters where mistaking dependence
  for competence is dangerous.
- **Dataset / stimulus hygiene and scoping.** All stimuli are public, DE-IDENTIFIED
  datasets (UCI Mushroom [29], CUB-200-2011 [30], HAM10000 / ISIC [31]) containing no
  PII; participant response data is pseudonymized, access-controlled, and retained per
  IRB / data-protection policy. Mushroom-edibility and dermoscopy categories are used
  strictly as RESEARCH STIMULI with dataset ground-truth labels - NOT foraging or
  clinical guidance.

### Readouts / metrics
- **C1** = unaided DELAYED post-test accuracy (AI + explanation withdrawn, ~1 week):
  the only durable, AI-independent competence a learning system may claim.
- **C2** = assisted-minus-unaided accuracy gap at MATCHED time (transient
  scaffolding/reliance; can be net-negative = crutch).
- **C3** = participant x item-difficulty x explanation-type INTERACTION variance
  component (scaffold-by-difficulty; reliance built on items the novice cannot solve
  alone). SEPARATELY LABELED.
- **Source metric for B1/collapse:** assisted post-test accuracy AND the
  agreement-based appropriate-reliance rate (the source's own scalars).
- **Supporting:** cognitive load (NASA-TLX [32], 6 subscales -- also the
  load-matching check for Leg-1); calibration of self-confidence (confidence-accuracy
  gap / Brier score); time-on-task; transfer items (novel unseen instances); per-item
  difficulty.
- **Retention slope:** immediate-versus-delayed change, reported per arm and per dose
  (the dissociation lives here).

### Estimator
- **Core model:** a crossed GENERALIZED LINEAR MIXED-EFFECTS MODEL (GLMM), binomial
  family for item-level correctness (correct/incorrect), with crossed random effects
  for participant, item, explanation-type, AND the participant x item x explanation-
  type interaction; fixed effects for AI-presence-at-test, test-time, dose, and their
  interactions. Fit by Laplace approximation / adaptive Gauss-Hermite quadrature
  (e.g., lme4::glmer [33] in R, Julia MixedModels.jl, or a Bayesian variant in brms /
  Stan); REML used for any companion LMM on continuous outcomes (e.g., calibration,
  NASA-TLX). Variance components map to C1/C2/C3 with parametric-bootstrap or
  profile-likelihood confidence intervals.
- **COLLAPSE UNIT TEST (by-construction identity):** in code, (1) DISABLE the
  AI-removed channel (evaluate the AI-present assisted post-test, delay = 0),
  (2) set placebo/counterfactual-explanation weight = 0 (real explanation only),
  (3) marginalize the variance decomposition to a single grand-mean readout (uniform
  weights, no participant/item/explanation-type split). Assert the profile recomputes
  the source assisted-accuracy / appropriate-reliance scalar AS RECOMPUTED from the
  same data WITHIN FLOATING-POINT TOLERANCE. Matching the source's PUBLISHED scalar
  is the separate B1 check, asserted only WITHIN REPRODUCTION TOLERANCE (never exact).

### Baseline ladder B1-B4 (mapped to the runs)
- **B1 (collapse target = source recomputed):** run AI-present + real explanation,
  immediate, full dose; compute assisted accuracy / appropriate-reliance our way.
  Equals the collapse unit-test output within floating-point tolerance; reproduces
  the source's reported measurement within reproduction tolerance.
- **B2 (AI-removed unaided IMMEDIATE correction):** withdraw AI + explanation at the
  immediate test, measure unaided. The single most important fix; first separation of
  durable C1 from transient C2 (before the delay is added).
- **B3 (full transfer-controlled, reliance-decomposed profile):** the full factorial
  -- AI-removed unaided DELAYED post-test as the C1 readout, crossed with
  real-vs-placebo explanation and the AI-present-vs-removed factor; the crossed GLMM
  emits C1, C2, and the separately labeled C3. This is the full operating object.
- **B4 (ORACLE gold-pedagogy ceiling -- UPPER BOUND ONLY):** gold instruction
  (human-expert tutoring, full worked solutions, or training under ground-truth-
  correct error-free explanations) read on the SAME unaided delayed post-test; bounds
  the maximum durable C1 the task/explanation channel could ever deliver. Upper bound
  only -- the within-study real-vs-placebo and AI-present-vs-removed contrasts remain
  the PRIMARY estimator; the secondary-domain (CUB) generalization gap is likewise an
  upper bound, not primary.

### Falsifiability triad (restated, with predicted dissociation)
1. **By-construction collapse:** the unit test above; if the profile does NOT recompute
   the assisted scalar within floating-point tolerance, the construction is wrong.
2. **DUAL placebo (both legs required):**
   - *Leg-1 (STRUCTURE-DESTROYING):* content-scrambled explanation matched on surface
     form, length, and cognitive-load footprint but with no veridical task signal. If
     the measured durable C1 was real, C1 must collapse toward zero under Leg-1
     (isolating any pure surface-engagement effect).
   - *Leg-2 (ABSENT-MECHANISM):* remove the AI's predictive advice while keeping an
     explanation-shaped scaffold (no correct answer to anchor on), or a matched-time
     self-study control. C2 must drop to approximately zero (no AI advice to rely on).
3. **Dose-response monotonicity:** as explanation dose goes none -> minimal -> full,
   the ASSISTED scalar rises monotonically while the UNAIDED delayed C1 fails to rise
   commensurately and/or C2 and C3 rise monotonically -- a measurable assisted-vs-
   durable DISSOCIATION.
- **Predicted pattern if RCLG holds:** assisted accuracy / appropriate-reliance rises
  with dose; unaided delayed C1 flat or falling; C2 (and C3 on hard items) rising;
  Leg-1 drives C1 toward zero; Leg-2 drives C2 toward zero.
- **Pattern that REFUTES us (state it plainly):** unaided delayed C1 tracks the
  assisted scalar one-for-one, BOTH placebo legs come back null, and there is no dose
  dissociation -- i.e., the (X)AI learning gain is genuinely durable and we are wrong.

### Power / feasibility
- **A-priori power by SIMULATION** (Monte Carlo from the fitted GLMM; e.g., R simr,
  or Julia MixedModels + MixedModelsSim) for (i) the C3 interaction variance component
  and (ii) the dose slope. Report the MINIMUM DETECTABLE C3 (smallest explanation-type-
  by-difficulty interaction / difficulty-slope difference detectable at 80% power) and
  the minimum detectable dose slope.
- **Target N (order of magnitude):** a few hundred participants across arms -- roughly
  300-600 total (on the order of ~100 per between-subject explanation-type arm, sized
  up for the C3 interaction). Vetted crowdsourcing/lab panel (e.g., Prolific) or a
  lab pool.
- **Item bank:** roughly 60-80 items per domain, split into difficulty-matched
  parallel forms (~20 items per occasion) so train / immediate / delayed pools are
  disjoint and include dedicated transfer items.
- **Session length:** session 1 ~ training 30-45 min + immediate test ~15 min; session
  2 (+~1 week) delayed test ~15-20 min. About 1.25-1.5 participant-hours each.
- **Cost is PARTICIPANT-HOURS, not GPU-hours.** Primary domain: on the order of
  ~500-700 participant-hours. Classifier-training compute is MODEST and one-time:
  UCI Mushroom = trivial CPU (decision tree / logistic model, attributions in
  seconds); CUB / HAM10000 = a single-GPU fine-tune of a standard backbone (a few
  GPU-hours, one-time, LOW GPU). No large GPU budget.

### Expected outcomes
- **Headline prediction:** a SUBSTANTIAL fraction of the reported (X)AI learning gain
  is C2/C3 (transient reliance-scaffolding and its scaffold-by-difficulty
  interaction), with the assisted scalar DISSOCIATING from durable unaided C1.
- **Crutch signature:** conditions where richer explanation (higher dose) IMPROVES the
  assisted number but FLATTENS or REDUCES durable C1 -- apparent teaching that is
  really dependence, concentrated (via C3) on the hard items the novice cannot solve
  alone.
- **Identifiability-bounded claims:** C1 and C2 are estimated as primary; C3 is
  reported with its interval and may be only BOUNDED at feasible N (per Q3(g)) -- we do
  not overclaim its magnitude.
- **Refutation result (pre-committed):** if unaided delayed C1 tracks the assisted
  scalar, both placebo legs are null, and there is no dose dissociation, RCLG is
  refuted and the source's durable-learning claim stands.
